item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly, this is a pretty good scip, and I love the way it is handled, but I do have one problem.
Maybe it's just me, but I feel like some of the dialogue in the interview between Morlo and Austin kinda reads like Austin is new to the English language. Some words don't fit in the sentence, or what he says just sounds kinda off. Maybe just read through and clean up some of the sentence structure. One in particular is when he says "Ah, you have never changed ever since then, haven't you?" This sentence just sounds strange. There are a couple others as well, but this is the one that I had to read like five times because it just made so little sense in the way he says it.
Overall, a good +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I see. I'm gonna try to see what I could further tighten up in the first parts of the article. In any case, I'm glad you enjoyed this one, and found the formatting to be good :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks! I'm glad you enjoyed this one :)

I was mainly eyeballing the revenue statistics based on what I could gather from google about Coke's profits, and then comparing them with the company's performance in recent years :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The pandas themselves weren't especially interesting to read about, and a bunch of the first 75% could probably have been cut, but that great dialogue absolutely clinched the +1 from me. Also appreciated the elegant formatting on those collapsible docs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Before I read the SCP, I made a guess on its content:

"Let me guess: 5840 concerns the commercialization of an endangered species, and the failure of the communist dream in China and its subversion by Deng Xiaoping's state capitalism"

I was half right there.

Big improvement from your Pepsi Soviet Subs: greater cohesion between pandabots, Coke's anomalous division, and a deal between the Foundation and Coke. Especially in how it questions how effective the Foundation is in containing SCPs if it is beholden to monetary interests as well (something I do want to explore in the future, though much more blatantly)

+1

Though I wonder, where did you get those revenue statistics from?

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
This is Veralta, once again bringing you a tale about roaring and domineering capitalism, propaganda, and monetary dreams against a backdrop of reality, all with pandas!

Special thanks to DrAkimoto, DrObscurus, Naveil, CaptainCold, Nagiros, Marcelles_Raynes, Meska, Placeholder McD, DrUnoriginal, JakDragonX for helping me make this skip the way it is now :)
